name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. The Now Platform connects systems, data, and processes with AI agents to eliminate friction in enterprise operations. ServiceNow serves over 8,100 customers including 85% of the Fortune 500, generating $3.77B in Q1 2026 total revenue at a 32% non-GAAP operating margin. The company completed a 5-for-1 stock split in December 2025.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has executed one of the most successful platform transformations in enterprise software history — from ITSM ticketing (2004-2015) to enterprise workflow platform (2015-2020) to AI-native enterprise platform (2020-present), expanding TAM from ~$20B to $220B+ [1][2]. Each pivot redefined the category rather than defended the legacy. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist customers spending over $1M in annual contract value grew more than 130% year-over-year in Q1 2026, representing measurable, quantifiable AI revenue contribution [1]. The company's agentic AI roadmap is shipping production features — not vaporware — with autonomous workflow execution across IT, HR, and customer service. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow is the undisputed category leader in enterprise workflow orchestration with no close independent competitor. 85% of the Fortune 500 are customers, and current remaining performance obligations of $12.64B (up 22.5% YoY) demonstrate deepening customer commitment [1]. Pipeline (2/2): The Armis cybersecurity acquisition is expected to close H2 2026, adding security operations to the platform and contributing ~1% incremental revenue [2]. The agentic AI roadmap includes specific named products and capabilities with launch timelines. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending is at or near sector average for enterprise SaaS, not top quartile. Total: 9.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose — 'make the world work better for everyone' — is specific, thoughtful, and genuinely connected to a larger societal benefit: removing friction from work so humans can focus on higher-value activities [3]. The mission is clearly lived out through platform expansion from IT to HR to customer service to security, each extension making enterprise work function better. It differentiates from peers and shapes product decisions. However, it lacks the multi-decade validation of a transformative mission (the company was founded in 2004), preventing a score of 4. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofits and underserved communities. The company has committed to carbon neutrality and was named to Ethisphere's 2026 World's Most Ethical Companies list [3]. General corporate philanthropy with some distinctive programs. Employee Retention (1/2): ServiceNow has grown to 29,000+ employees under McDermott's leadership. The company's 'human-AI' narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — and the strong cultural emphasis on inclusion support retention, but specific turnover or tenure data is not publicly available [3]. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO Bill McDermott purchased $3M of ServiceNow shares in open-market transactions in February 2026, and multiple senior executives terminated their Rule 10b5-1 pre-planned selling agreements, cancelling all future pre-scheduled share sales [4]. This shift from programmatic selling to direct buying represents exceptional insider conviction. Total: 3 + 1 + 1 + 2 = 7.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics using the Growth stock weighting (10 total weight units). Liquidity check: avg_daily_volume (26,937,394) x stock_price ($103.08) = $2.78B — passes the $1M liquidity floor by a wide margin. Core metrics: D/E of 20.73% is exceptionally low for a software company, scoring 1.0 (target ≤70%). ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target, scoring 1.0 at 2x weight. Revenue growth YoY of 22.1% well exceeds the 10% target, scoring 1.0. Revenue growth 5yr CAGR of ~23% scores 1.0 at 2x weight. Forward P/E of 20.51 is nearly at the 20 target, scoring 0.99. GAAP profit margin of 12.6% is the primary weakness — below the 20% target but well above the 0% floor, scoring 0.63 (non-GAAP operating margin is 32%, reflecting heavy stock-based compensation typical of high-growth software). Forward PEG of ~1.0 (forward P/E 20.51 / ~20% long-term earnings growth) scores 1.0 at 2x weight. Weighted sum: 9.62 / 10.0 = infrastructure score of 10 [1][5].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): ServiceNow occupies one of the most AI-resilient positions in enterprise software — it IS the execution and orchestration layer that AI agents require to function in enterprise environments. CEO McDermott's thesis is precise and validated: 'AI excels at identifying problems and providing advice but cannot execute solutions' — ServiceNow controls the execution layer that translates AI outputs into auditable, compliant enterprise actions across 80 billion+ annual workflows [2][3]. JPMorgan has explicitly identified ServiceNow as 'AI-resilient,' citing its role as connective tissue integrating AI insights into reliable enterprise processes. The platform's deep integration into customer IT, HR, security, and customer service workflows creates massive switching costs — average contract values are expanding, not contracting, as AI features are layered onto existing deployments. The primary risk is that agentic AI matures faster than anticipated and replaces workflow orchestration entirely, but the enterprise requirement for auditability, compliance, and cross-system coordination makes this scenario unlikely in the medium term. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist customers spending over $1M in ACV grew more than 130% year-over-year in Q1 2026, representing quantifiable, material AI revenue [1]. ServiceNow is not just protected from AI — it is monetizing AI directly through its platform. Total: 8 + 1 = 9.

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott operates ServiceNow with a genuinely infinite mindset. His 'Rule of 55+' discipline (revenue growth + FCF margin must exceed 55) prioritizes long-term sustainable performance over short-term growth at any cost. The contract extension through 2030, the $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sell-off, and the termination of executive selling plans all signal a leadership team playing to keep playing. The company's three-phase platform transformation — ITSM to enterprise workflows to AI platform — demonstrates existential flexibility in service of a consistent cause: making work work better.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): ServiceNow's cause — 'make the world work better for everyone' by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific. It describes a future state (frictionless enterprise workflows powered by AI agents) that doesn't fully exist yet, and it invites both technology partners and customers to contribute [3]. The cause has been validated through the company's consistent platform expansion: each new module (HR, security, customer service) extends the same purpose into new domains. McDermott articulates this cause consistently across economic cycles and competitive environments. However, ServiceNow was founded only in 2004 and the current cause has been tested through just one significant market disruption (the 2026 AI sell-off). It has not yet survived a leadership transition or multi-decade execution. Score: 2 points, display 7.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): ServiceNow was named to Ethisphere's 2026 World's Most Ethical Companies list, reflecting organizational commitment to ethical practices [3]. McDermott's leadership team has remained largely stable, and the company's 'human-AI' narrative — positioning AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — creates psychological safety around the most existential question in tech today. The company has grown to 29,000+ employees while maintaining cultural cohesion. However, specific employee tenure, voluntary turnover, or psychological safety data is not publicly available, and the absence of concrete metrics prevents a score of 2. Score: 1 point, display 6.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP (his former company), and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose strengths push ServiceNow to improve its platform capabilities [2]. The company's competitive language consistently focuses on building the best AI workflow platform, not on defeating specific competitors. The acquisitions of Moveworks (AI customer service) and Armis (security) reflect competitive awareness — rivals' strengths revealing ServiceNow's portfolio gaps rather than defensive moves to block competitors. Score: 1 point, display 10.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): ServiceNow's transformation from ITSM ticketing system (2004-2015) to enterprise workflow platform (2015-2020) to AI-native enterprise platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility executed three times in two decades [2]. Each pivot was driven by the Just Cause — making work work better — not by desperation. The company didn't defend its ITSM monopoly when the opportunity to become an enterprise AI platform emerged; it redefined the category, expanding TAM from ~$20B to $220B+. The willingness to brand itself as the 'Defining AI Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century' while still generating 100% of revenue from workflow subscriptions reflects forward-looking flexibility. Score: 2 points, display 10.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): McDermott's actions during the January-February 2026 software sector rout — when IGV fell 23% and ServiceNow dropped on AI disruption fears — define Sinek's concept of courage. He purchased $3M of ServiceNow stock personally, terminated his pre-planned selling agreement, and persuaded other senior executives to do the same [4]. This is leadership putting personal capital at risk in service of the Just Cause when the market is questioning the company's future. The 'Rule of 55+' profitability standard maintains capital discipline even when it means forgoing faster growth. The contract extension through 2030 signals a leader committed to the long game, not positioning for an exit. Score: 2 points, display 10.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow delivered a strong Q1 2026 with $3.77B total revenue (up 22% YoY), subscription revenue of $3.67B (up 22%), and free cash flow of $1.67B at a 44% margin [1]. Current remaining performance obligations of $12.64B grew 22.5% YoY, and total RPO of $27.7B grew 25% — both acceleration metrics signaling sustained demand. The AI monetization story is materializing: Now Assist customers spending over $1M in ACV grew more than 130% year-over-year, validating McDermott's thesis that ServiceNow is the execution and orchestration layer that enterprise AI agents require [1][2]. With full-year 2026 subscription revenue guidance of $15.53-15.57B (20.5-21% growth), a 32% non-GAAP operating margin, and $2.2B in share repurchases in Q1 alone, ServiceNow is delivering the rare combination of 20%+ growth with expanding profitability at scale. The post-split valuation (forward P/E ~20.5x, PEG ~1.0x) is the most attractive entry point the stock has offered since the 2022 correction.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on execution risk at scale and the durability of the AI premium. While the forward P/E of 20.5x appears reasonable, the trailing P/E of 61.4x reflects heavy stock-based compensation that depresses GAAP earnings — SBC represents ~19% of revenue, meaning real economic costs are significantly higher than non-GAAP metrics suggest [5]. The GAAP profit margin of 12.6% is below target for a company of this maturity. The Armis acquisition (closing H2 2026) adds integration complexity and ~1% revenue contribution against meaningful execution risk. More fundamentally, the agentic AI thesis cuts both ways: if AI agents become capable enough to orchestrate their own workflows without a centralized platform, ServiceNow's 'execution layer' moat could erode faster than the market anticipates. The rapid growth of AI-native competitors building workflow tools from scratch — rather than layering AI onto a legacy platform — represents a longer-term structural risk that the current valuation does not fully discount.
key_catalysts: Now Assist AI monetization accelerating with >130% YoY growth in $1M+ ACV customers, potentially contributing 3-5% of incremental subscription revenue by year-end 2026 as enterprise AI adoption expands
Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing H2 2026, adding security operations to the platform and expanding addressable market while demonstrating the 'AI control tower' integration strategy

Forward P/E compression to ~20.5x post-split creating the most attractive risk/reward entry point since 2022, with potential for multiple re-expansion as AI revenue contribution becomes more visible in reported results
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to orchestrate enterprise workflows autonomously within 2-3 years, rendering ServiceNow's centralized platform architecture a legacy layer rather than a necessary execution tier

Stock-based compensation at ~19% of revenue masking true economic profitability — GAAP profit margin of 12.6% versus 32% non-GAAP operating margin creates a significant gap that could compress real returns if SBC does not moderate as growth slows
Armis integration complexity and CFO transition dynamics creating execution risk in a stock where any single-quarter miss against 20%+ growth expectations could trigger material multiple compression

ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott AI Strategy Commentary and Share Purchase, February-March 2026
ServiceNow Corporate Website, Ethisphere 2026 Most Ethical Companies, ServiceNow.org Impact Report
